ground_truth.title: US News MBA Rankings: Full & Part Time Programs - Stacy Blackman Consulting - MBA Admissions Consulting
ground_truth.main_content: MBA ranking reports in aggregate can help an applicant to see trends around MBA program brand and prioritize their reach versus reasonable program options. US News & World Report has been the ranking king and so we present its data here. The 2023-2024 US News ranking data for the top 20 full-time MBA programs can be found in the chart below. See details and dates on application deadlines. The top full-time programs include Chicago Booth School of Business ranked #1, followed by Kellogg School of Management at #2, and UPenn Wharton School at #3. Harvard Business School holds the #5 spot with a 14% acceptance rate and average GMAT of 727. Stanford Graduate School of Business has a 9% acceptance rate, the most selective among top programs, with the highest median starting salary at $182,272. Dartmouth Tuck School of Business moved up significantly from #11 to #6 (tie) in the 2023 rankings. For part-time programs, UC Berkeley Haas School of Business leads at #1, followed by Chicago Booth at #2 and Kellogg at #3. Part-time programs show much higher acceptance rates, typically ranging from 61% to 93%. Request a free consultation with a Principal on our SBC team to assess school fit and admit chances.
